etymology.description: Phonetic 歬 (qián, "phonetic element") + semantic 刀(刀) (dāo, "knife") = original form of 剪(剪) (jiǎn, "to cut")
etymology.interpretation: In its ancient usage, 歬(qián, "phonetic element") served as the phonetic component in characters such as 剪(剪) (jiǎn, "to cut"), where the semantic component 刀(刀) conveyed the cutting action. The transformation of 止(qián, "foot") to 䒑 and 舟(qián, "boat") to 月 in 后 forms shows the phonetic element's transliteration. Today, 歬 is mainly a phonetic element and is less commonly used as an independent character.
